message:
test(fcp-conformance/admission-gc): pin gc_stale_peers + record_auth_failure (br-iei8u)

Two AdmissionController surfaces complementing br-d2uly (byte/symbol
budgets) and br-uekoy (decode-slot + amplification): record_auth_failure
(credential-stuffing brute-force defense at the admission layer) and
gc_stale_peers (periodic memory-bound cleanup that prevents the
per-peer table from growing unboundedly across attacker-controlled
NodeIds).

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_mesh::admission API:

- record_auth_failure_succeeds_under_budget
- record_auth_failure_above_budget_returns_retry_after (the
  AuthFailureBudgetExceeded back-pressure signal — retry_after > 0
  prevents tight retry loops on credential stuffing)
- record_auth_failure_per_peer_isolation (a noisy peer hitting
  the cap MUST NOT rate-limit polite peers by association)
- record_auth_failure_does_not_allocate_for_unrelated_peer
- gc_stale_peers_removes_idle_old_peers
- gc_stale_peers_keeps_recent_peers (within staleness threshold)
- gc_stale_peers_keeps_peers_with_inflight_decodes_regardless_of_staleness
  (CRITICAL: an active decoder MUST be kept across GC even when its
  window is stale — losing inflight_decodes would silently corrupt
  decode tracking and let the peer over-allocate slots)
- gc_stale_peers_evicts_after_release_when_window_is_stale (once
  inflight=0 AND window stale, peer is eligible)
- gc_stale_peers_on_empty_table_is_a_no_op
- gc_stale_peers_preserves_other_peers_when_one_is_evicted (peer-
  specific eviction, not table-wide)

Mid-stream fix: the preserves_other test originally used a 1-sec
threshold but both peers were past that — corrected to 30-sec
threshold so fresh (10s old) is kept and stale (60s old) is evicted.

All 10 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
admission_gc_and_auth_failure_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
